title: Drymophloeus angustifolius
intro: Drymophloeus angustifolius, commonly known as the Sapo tree, is a species of plant in the family Arecaceae, native to the tropical forests of West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Drymophloeus angustifolius include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of a traditional soap made from its sap, hence its common name.
taxonomy: Drymophloeus angustifolius is classified within the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family. This classification places it among the monocotyledonous flowering plants.
